metadata.title: WCN — Global Institutional Orchestrator
metadata.description: WCN turns fragmented capital, resources, and execution into a structured, verifiable, settleable global network for Web3 and AI.
metadata.ogDescription: Coordination layer for the decentralized economy: nodes, deals, proof, and settlement.
metadata.twitterDescription: Structured coordination for institutional Web3 and AI — verifiable work, aligned settlement.

footer.tagline: Global Institutional Orchestrator for Web3 & AI. Turning fragmented capital and execution into a verifiable, settleable network.

home.eyebrow: Global Institutional Orchestrator for Web3 & AI
home.headline: Build the coordination layer of the decentralized economy.
home.lede: WCN turns fragmented capital, resources, and execution into a structured, verifiable, settleable global network.
home.pobDesc: Proof of Business that can be verified
home.designedDesc: A simple structure that scales: define opportunity, verify contribution, then settle.
home.clearPrimitivesDesc: Node, Deal, Task, Proof, Settlement — one vocabulary across the network.
home.verifiableWorkDesc: Proof is the unit of trust. Without verification, allocation can't be fair.
home.alignedIncentivesDesc: Settlement follows contribution. Less noise, more outcomes.
home.trustedByDesc: Designed for capital partners, regional operators, and execution teams across Web3 and AI.
home.loopDesc: Every opportunity becomes a workflow. Every workflow becomes proof. Every proof becomes the basis of settlement.
home.testimonial1: WCN finally gives us a structured way to verify cross-border execution and settle fairly.
home.testimonial2: The PoB model changed how we think about contribution tracking. Evidence-first is the only way.
home.testimonial3: Agent execution with audit trails means our AI tools integrate cleanly into institutional workflows.

about.title: About
about.metaDesc: WCN is a global business network: nodes organize resources, agents scale execution, PoB verifies outcomes, and settlement aligns with contribution—not hype.
about.headline: Institutional operating system,<linebreak></linebreak>not another platform.
about.lede: Web3 Capital Network is a business network layer for the Web3 and AI economy—where resources meet execution, outcomes meet proof, and future settlement can trace back to what actually happened.
about.subLede: Wiki chapter 1 frames the same idea: WCN does not start from "launch a chain and hope." It starts from organizing real participants, tasks, and evidence—then lets identity, assets, and settlement attach to verifiable history.
about.pillarNodeBody: Operators who bring capital, projects, services, media, and regional reach—and accept responsibility for what they route into the system.
about.pillarAgentBody: Research, outreach, monitoring, and ops that plug into tasks under permissions and logs—not chatbots bolted on the side.
about.pillarPobBody: Only closed, evidenced loops earn ledger presence. PoB is the gate between operations and long-term value, not an activity score.
about.definitionDesc: Aligned with project-intro and boundary chapters: say the constructive definition first, then remove the six recurring misconceptions before they distort everything downstream.
about.whatWcnIsLede: A coordination infrastructure that aligns capital, resources, execution, proof, and—over time—settlement on verified contribution.
about.whatWcnIsNotLede: If any of these are what you are looking for, WCN will feel misaligned—and that is useful to know early.
about.whyNowDesc: From wiki §1.4: timing is not "because the acronym is trendy"—several structural shifts now land together, and WCN is built to respond to all of them.
about.diffTitle: Structural stack, not a feature slide
about.diffDesc: Chapter 13.4's point in one line: few teams wire resource organization, execution scale, proof, and long-term settlement into the same accountable chain—WCN does.
about.measureDesc: Adapted from wiki §1.1: WCN cares who moved the outcome—not who was loudest in the channel.
about.foundationDesc: Operating defaults drawn from the same judgment set as solution chapter 3—coordination, proof, settlement, and responsibility beat aggregation and narrative alone.
about.ctaDesc: Solution architecture, node system, PoB, and "why WCN" chapters unpack the same story with chapter-level rigor—start from intro or jump to differentiation.
about.whatIsBullet1: Coordinates projects, capital, services, distribution, and execution in one workflow—not a passive directory.
about.whatIsBullet2: Starts from business density and accountable nodes; chain identity and assets follow when the network earns them.
about.whatIsBullet3: Combines human judgment with Agent scale, with evidence and review before outcomes harden into PoB.
about.whatIsNotBullet1: Not an L1 launched to "find use cases later"—infrastructure follows real loops.
about.whatIsNotBullet2: Not a points-for-posting community; noise does not enter the value layer.
about.whatIsNotBullet3: Not a single-purpose fund or placement shop—many loop types can qualify when verified.
about.whatIsNotBullet4: Not open "buy a badge" membership—nodes are reviewed responsibility seats.
about.whatIsNotBullet5: Not a yield or guaranteed-return token story at this stage.
about.whatIsNotBullet6: Not buzzword stacking—every layer answers who did the work, what was proven, and how credit is attributed.
about.window1Body: Competition shifted from "new chain, new model" to whether teams can organize capital, markets, and execution end to end.
about.window2Body: AI can execute, but without attributable tasks, evidence, and PoB, outputs never become settleable objects.
about.window3Body: Capital and partners increasingly ask what actually shipped—not impressions, narratives, or vanity metrics alone.
about.window4Body: RWA, stablecoins, and institutional-grade expectations make on-chain carry credible once proof exists.
about.window5Body: Tooling is mature enough to stand up nodes, tasks, evidence, and PoB before forcing token-led distribution.
about.struct1Body: Not anonymous users—parties that introduce resources, advance deals, and own scope inside the network.
about.struct2Body: Scoped automation inside tasks and audit trails, amplifying humans instead of replacing accountability.
about.struct3Body: Verified outcomes become durable records and the structured input for reputation and later settlement design.
about.struct4Body: Phase 2+ allocation, staking, and governance extend the same contribution logic—without decoupling from evidence.
about.principle1Body: WCN aligns participants around executable loops—not another list of logos that never ships together.
about.principle2Body: Claims need evidence, review, and attribution. Without that, coordination collapses into marketing noise.
about.principle3Body: Long-term allocation should track verified contribution curves, not whoever narrates loudest this quarter.
about.principle4Body: Every node carries real boundaries: what they route in, what they execute, and what they are willing to stand behind in PoB.
about.measureStep2: Who advanced collaboration into structured work
about.measureStep4: What evidence exists and who reviewed it
about.measureStep5: Whether the result actually holds—not whether the thread was loud

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.metaDesc: WCN's business loop: resources enter through nodes, work is structured and executed with humans and agents, evidence is verified, PoB records outcomes, and settlement aligns with contribution.
howItWorks.headline: From resources to verified outcomes.
howItWorks.lede: WCN is not "activity rewards." It is a business loop: structure work, execute with humans and agents, verify with evidence, and record PoB as the basis for future settlement—so allocation follows contribution, not narrative alone.
howItWorks.subLede: Every step below is reflected in the product: nodes scope intake, tasks and deals carry execution, Proof Desk handles evidence, and PoB is the bridge from operations to long-term value and settlement design.
howItWorks.operatingLoopDesc: Skipping any step collapses the system into noise. WCN is built so closed loops are visible, reviewable, and attributable end to end.
howItWorks.archTitle: Five layers, narrowest at the base
howItWorks.archDesc: Traditional systems allocate by status or negotiation. WCN compresses trust into verified contribution—the stack is widest where humans and agents touch reality, and tightest where assets and settlement must not drift from proof.
howItWorks.bridgeEntryBody: Intake without a responsible node is just content. Nodes declare boundaries so tasks, evidence, and PoB can be traced to accountable operators.
howItWorks.bridgeProofBody: Outcomes earn a ledger presence only after evidence and review. That is what keeps settlement and reputation from decoupling from what actually happened.
howItWorks.ctaDesc: Apply as a node, or open the wiki for chapter-deep walkthroughs of tasks, agents, PoB, and governance.
howItWorks.loopStep1Body: Projects, capital, media, services, and regional relationships route through accountable nodes—not anonymous feeds.
howItWorks.loopStep2Body: Needs become tasks with owners, collaborators, milestones, and explicit definitions of done.
howItWorks.loopStep3Body: People carry judgment, trust, and negotiation; agents scale research, drafting, and operations under permission and logging.
howItWorks.loopStep4Body: Contracts, logs, dashboards, and on-chain references attach to outcomes and pass review before claims harden.
howItWorks.loopStep5Body: Only closed, verifiable loops become Proof of Business—an auditable ledger input, not a popularity score.
howItWorks.loopStep6Body: Verified PoB competes for periodic allocation; governance and staking extend the same contribution logic.
howItWorks.layer1Detail: Where capital, deals, and services enter with a named responsible party.
howItWorks.layer2Detail: Scoped automation that must remain attributable and auditable.
howItWorks.layer3Detail: Proof Desk and policies decide what the system is allowed to remember.
howItWorks.layer4Detail: Phase 2+ mechanics tie distribution to verified contribution curves.
howItWorks.layer5Detail: Long-term instruments and liquidity sit on verified history—not raw hype.

nodes.title: Node Network — WCN
nodes.metaDesc: Nodes are WCN responsibility units: resource intake, execution, evidence, PoB, and settlement accountability—with tiered scope and a full admission lifecycle.
nodes.headline: Nodes are responsibility units—not labels.
nodes.lede: In WCN, a node is the accountable surface for intake, execution, evidence, and settlement. Tiers describe scope and trust, upgraded by verified PoB, collaboration quality, and risk history—not by vanity metrics.
nodes.subLede: This matches the operating loop in our product spec: structure work, execute with humans and agents, verify with evidence, record PoB, and align settlement to contribution. Nodes are where that loop lands in the real economy.
nodes.hierarchyDesc: Tiers are not hype bands. They express how much of the network a node is trusted to carry—from global anchors to scoped agent execution.
nodes.lifecycleDesc: Aligned with the node console lifecycle in our implementation roadmap: application, review, contract and onboarding, operations, and ongoing tier or status changes.
nodes.ctaTitle: Ready to operate as a node?
nodes.ctaDesc: Submit an application, or read the wiki for deeper architecture, PoB, and settlement context.
nodes.resp1Body: Projects, capital, media, services, and regional flow enter the network through declared node boundaries—not anonymous feeds.
nodes.resp2Body: Nodes structure and own work: tasks, collaborators, milestones, and delivery risk within their scope.
nodes.resp3Body: Outcomes attach verifiable artifacts; closed loops can become Proof of Business (PoB) records for review and allocation logic.
nodes.resp4Body: Nodes carry settlement responsibility: honoring allocation outcomes, fees, and governance where applicable—bypass is a breach, not a feature.
nodes.tier1Desc: Cross-region capital, flagship projects, and strategic infrastructure connectors that anchor network-wide trust and liquidity.
nodes.tier2Desc: National or financial-center hubs for compliance, capital formation, distribution, and regional deal orchestration.
nodes.tier3Desc: Local ecosystem operators and vertical specialists—AI, RWA, stablecoins, media, and technical delivery close to the ground.
nodes.tier4Desc: Market makers, legal, audit, brand, and engineering partners that plug in as accountable service units.
nodes.tier5Desc: Registered agents executing scoped tasks under permission, logging, and audit—human judgment stays in the loop where required.
nodes.life1Body: Identity, capability, jurisdiction, and resource boundaries—plus optional seat or stake alignment where the program requires it.
nodes.life2Body: Verification, risk screening, and clarifications; probation or conditional admission when the network needs more signal.
nodes.life3Body: Agreements, billing hooks, and go-live criteria so operational status is explicit—not implied by a profile alone.
nodes.life4Body: Tasks, deals, evidence, and PoB under the node's scope—with visibility rules that match role and confidentiality.
nodes.life5Body: Upgrades from verified PoB volume, evidence quality, and collaboration; probation, suspension, or offboarding for sustained failure or severe breach.
nodes.govAdmissionText: Identity + capability + resource boundary + optional stake/seat binding when the node class requires it.
nodes.govUpgradeText: PoB volume, evidence quality, collaboration success, and compliance history—tiers reflect verified contribution, not marketing claims.
nodes.govRemovalText: Repeated low contribution, fabricated evidence, severe breach, or systematic settlement bypass.
nodes.areBullet1: Accountable routing points for capital, projects, and services
nodes.areBullet2: Owners of execution risk inside their declared territory
nodes.areBullet3: Parties to evidence, review, and PoB where outcomes are claimed
nodes.areBullet4: Participants in settlement and fee logic tied to real contribution
nodes.notBullet1: Passive directory listings or unverified "partner" badges
nodes.notBullet2: Anonymous coordination channels without identity and boundaries
nodes.notBullet3: Short-term campaign shells with no ongoing settlement role
nodes.notBullet4: Substitutes for legal, tax, or regulatory obligations in your jurisdiction

pob.title: Proof of Business (PoB)
pob.metaDesc: PoB is WCN's result-first value layer: verified business loops, evidence, review, attribution, and an auditable path to settlement—not activity points.
pob.headline: Only verified outcomes become value.
pob.lede: PoB is WCN's result-first accounting layer. It does not pay for noise. It recognizes business loops that can be proved, attributed, and audited—so capital and execution align with what actually happened.
pob.subLede: This matches the wiki definition: PoB is not loyalty points or a marketing scoreboard. It is the mechanism that turns real, verifiable results into objects the system can admit, record, and—over time—use as settlement input.
pob.boundariesDesc: PoB rewards closed loops, not vanity metrics. If it cannot be evidenced and reviewed, it does not enter the ledger.
pob.verificationDesc: Aligned with the five-step path in our wiki: submission, evidence, review, conclusion, and archival—not a single click or social post.
pob.proofDeskDesc: Proof Desk is not a passive upload folder. It is where evidence is collected, reviews are queued, disputes stay visible, and final decisions are issued before anything becomes PoB.
pob.intakeDesc: Structured evidence packages tied to events
pob.disputesDesc: Space to contest outcomes or attribution
pob.conclusionsDesc: Explicit pass / more info / reject paths
pob.scoringDesc: A compact formula expresses how verified outcomes aggregate. Parameters evolve with governance; Phase 1 stresses evidence and auditability before token settlement.
pob.scoringFoot: Phase 1 focuses on evidence, review discipline, and ledger integrity. Token settlement and broader governance hooks land in Phase 2+.
pob.attributionBody: Without attribution, PoB only proves that a result exists. With it, the network can see who materially advanced the outcome—critical for fair credit, disputes, and future allocation.
pob.notLawBody: PoB is an operational and evidentiary layer inside WCN. It does not replace securities, tax, or licensing obligations in your jurisdiction—teams still need proper counsel and disclosures.
pob.ctaDesc: Start from nodes and tasks, or read the full PoB chapter in the wiki for definitions, attribution, and edge cases.
pob.pillar1Body: Only verified closed loops enter the PoB ledger. Activity without a provable outcome stays outside the value layer.
pob.pillar2Body: PoB distinguishes who led, who collaborated, and what actually made the result hold—not a flat “everyone participated” score.
pob.pillar3Body: Recorded PoB becomes the structured input for future allocation, fees, and governance—after evidence and auditability mature in Phase 1.
pob.pillar4Body: Validated outcomes persist as durable records instead of disappearing chat logs or campaign metrics.
pob.rewarded6: Agent execution loops (logged work that produces attributable results)
pob.notRewarded1: Posts, hype, and “activity for activity’s sake”
pob.notRewarded3: Bot traffic, wash trading, and circular attribution
pob.notRewarded4: Side deals that bypass system accounting and attribution
pob.verificationStep1Body: Register the closed loop with clear context: deal, task, nodes, and what “done” means for this outcome.
pob.verificationStep2Body: Upload structured proof—contracts, on-chain references, dashboards, emails, minutes, acceptance records.
pob.verificationStep3Body: Reviewers check authenticity, completeness, timeline, and attribution boundaries before a conclusion.
pob.verificationStep4Body: Approve, request more materials, reject, or route into disputes when outcomes or credit are contested.
pob.verificationStep5Body: Accepted records enter the PoB ledger as durable, queryable inputs for reputation and later settlement.
pob.factorBaseValueHint: Magnitude of the verified outcome tied to the loop.
pob.factorBusinessWeightHint: How strategically important the loop type is to the network.
pob.factorQualityMultHint: Evidence depth, confirmations, and audit readiness.
pob.factorTimeMultHint: Durability and how well the outcome holds over time.
pob.factorRiskDiscountHint: Disputes, anomalies, and policy flags reduce effective PoB.

auth.signInToAccount: Sign in to your WCN account
auth.oauthAccountLinked: An account with this email already exists. Please sign in with your original method.
auth.applyDesc: Submit your application to join the WCN network.
auth.signupSubtext: Create your account to get started

apply.metaDescription: Join the WCN network as a node. Submit your application to become a capital, project, service, or regional node.
apply.headline: Apply as a node.
apply.heroDesc: WCN is an invite-and-application network. Submit your information below and our team will review within 48 hours.
apply.step1Desc: Fill out the form with your background and resources.
apply.step2Desc: Our team evaluates fit, resources, and network alignment.
apply.step3Desc: Qualified applicants are invited to a 30-minute intro call.
apply.step4Desc: Seat assignment, Node NFT, permissions, and platform access.
apply.capitalDesc: VC, family office, fund, angel investor
apply.regionalDesc: Country lead, city hub, local network connector
apply.mediaDesc: Media outlet, KOL, community, event organizer
apply.industryDesc: Exchange, market maker, custodian, data provider
apply.tipMessage: Applications with specific resource descriptions and clear alignment with the WCN network have a significantly higher approval rate.
